patch.value: Persistent derived read index: serve list/context/next/get-by-filter from an O(result) index instead of scanning all item files

Core scale-out child: today every read enumerates .agents/pm/<type>/*.toon. Lands as SDK primitives (pm-usfg alignment) so CLI/MCP inherit. Duplicate check: semantic-index/ledger items (vector refresh ledger) cover embeddings only, not metadata reads.

Read commands consult a rebuildable, gitignored derived index (format per ADR pm-bl8x) maintained transactionally with item writes; index is never authoritative — pm can always rebuild it from .toon files and detects staleness automatically; benchmark shows list/context/next p95 < 1s at 1M items; falls back to full scan transparently when index is absent; zero behavior change to outputs

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Wave-2 entry scoping 2026-07-10 (planning pass). This item gates wave 2 (pm-3hps feature store depends on it), carries est 900m / risk high, and had no decomposition on record; staging it de-risks entry. Proposed slices, each independently landable with zero output change:
(S1) Rebuild + staleness substrate — gitignored derived-index directory, manifest keyed on (item file mtime+size+hash watermark, schema version, index format version per ADR pm-bl8x); `pm reindex` builds it; every read path detects staleness and transparently falls back to full scan. No read path consumes the index yet; ships the benchmark harness (synthetic 10k/100k/1M item corpora) so later slices have a regression baseline. This substrate is the shared foundation pm-3hps's signal store also needs (its design note already commits to derived/rebuildable) — landing S1 first lets wave-2 items proceed in parallel afterwards.
(S2) list/get-by-filter served from the index — the pure metadata projections (status/type/tags/priority/updated_at windows) with p95 target at 1M items; scan fallback proves equivalence in tests (index-on vs index-off golden outputs).
(S3) context/next served from the index — join with claim state and dep edges; blocked-companion counts come from the same edge table (fixes the class of GH-489 drift at the source).
(S4) Transactional maintenance on the write path — mutations update the index in the same commit boundary as the .toon write, with crash-safety = staleness detection from S1 (worst case: rebuild). Only after S4 does the index become continuously warm; S1-S3 tolerate lazy rebuild.
Decision needed at wave-2 entry (maintainer or synthesis pass): accept this slicing and start S1, or descope wave 2 to pm-3hps-on-scan (feature store computes signals via full scan first, index adoption later). Recommendation: S1 first — it is ~150-200m of the 900, unblocks parallelism, and avoids building the feature store twice.
